spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the accessibility case the chromestatus motivation calls out — user stylesheet flipping caret-animation back to auto.

interactive.rationale: Live editable input plus an override toggle that simulates user-stylesheet injection.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a user-side override against an author-styled animation; the demo does exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the override but doesn't demonstrate the proper author-side respect path (`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`) — only the heavy-handed `!important` user-stylesheet escape hatch.

references.rationale: Links the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5082469066604544 in see-also.

openQuestions.title: Show the author-respect alternative
openQuestions.detail: Pair the `!important` override with the cleaner @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) approach where the author voluntarily sets caret-animation: auto.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the user stylesheet install ceremony
openQuestions.detail: Real users can't easily inject CSS without an extension. Either link a guide to user stylesheets (Stylish, browser flags) or simulate it more clearly with a toggle that flips a class on body.

openQuestions.title: Cover photosensitive epilepsy explicitly
openQuestions.detail: Reference WCAG 2.3.1 / 2.3.2 thresholds (3 flashes/sec, red flash specs) explicitly so authors learn the practical bar.

summary: Sound a11y demo that ties the spec to a real user need. Strengthen by foregrounding the author-respect path (@media) before the heavy-handed user-override path.
